content:
# Exodus Chapter 30 -- The SOPHIA Bible (Consensus)
**Arbiter:** SOPHIA | **In council with:** EMET, LOGOS, PNEUMA
**Source Manuscript:** Leningrad Codex (B19A), Folios ~51v-52r (PDF pp. 101-104); Codex Vaticanus (Vat.gr.1209), pp. 73-74 (LXX) | **Source Image:** `source-texts/hebrew-OT/EXO_030_page_001.jpg`, `EXO_030_page_002.jpg`; `source-texts/greek-OT-LXX/EXO_030_vaticanus_page_001.jpg`, `EXO_030_vaticanus_page_002.jpg`

---

> **MANUSCRIPT NOTE -- THE SACRED MATERIALS OF WORSHIP:** The Leningrad Codex presents Exodus 30 across two folio pages in standard prose column format with full Masoretic vocalization and cantillation. This chapter gathers five distinct prescriptions -- the incense altar (vv. 1-10), the census ransom (vv. 11-16), the bronze basin (vv. 17-21), the anointing oil (vv. 22-33), and the sacred incense (vv. 34-38) -- each introduced by YHWH speaking to Moses. Where chapters 25-29 built the structure and consecrated its priests, chapter 30 supplies the remaining instruments and substances without which the tabernacle cannot function. The Vaticanus manuscript (pp. 73-74) follows the MT sequence closely throughout.

---

## The Altar of Incense (vv. 1-10)

**30:1** "And you shall make an altar, a place of burning incense; of acacia wood you shall make it. [fn1]

**30:2** A cubit its length and a cubit its width -- square it shall be -- and two cubits its height; from it shall be its horns. [fn2]

**30:3** And you shall overlay it with pure gold -- its top and its walls all around and its horns -- and you shall make for it a molding of gold all around. [fn3]

**30:4** And two rings of gold you shall make for it beneath its molding; on its two sides you shall make them, on its two flanks; and they shall be holders for poles to carry it by them. [fn4]

**30:5** And you shall make the poles of acacia wood, and you shall overlay them with gold. [fn5]

**30:6** And you shall place it before the veil which is over the ark of the testimony, before the mercy seat which is over the testimony, where I will meet with you there. [fn6]

**30:7** And Aaron shall burn upon it fragrant incense; morning by morning, when he tends the lamps, he shall burn it. [fn7]

**30:8** And when Aaron lights the lamps between the two evenings, he shall burn it -- a perpetual incense before YHWH throughout your generations. [fn8]

**30:9** You shall not offer upon it unauthorized incense, nor a burnt offering, nor a grain offering; and a drink offering you shall not pour upon it. [fn9]

**30:10** And Aaron shall make atonement upon its horns once in the year; from the blood of the sin offering of atonement, once in the year he shall make atonement upon it throughout your generations; it is most holy to YHWH." [fn10]

---

## The Census Ransom -- The Half-Shekel (vv. 11-16)

**30:11** And YHWH spoke to Moses, saying: [fn11]

**30:12** "When you take the sum of the Sons of Israel according to those who are numbered of them, then each man shall give a ransom for his life to YHWH when you number them, and there shall not be a plague among them when you number them. [fn12]

**30:13** This they shall give -- everyone who passes over to those who are numbered -- a half-shekel according to the shekel of the sanctuary (twenty gerahs the shekel); a half-shekel as a contribution to YHWH. [fn13]

**30:14** Everyone who passes over to those who are numbered, from a son of twenty years and upward, shall give the contribution of YHWH. [fn14]

**30:15** The rich shall not give more and the poor shall not give less than the half-shekel, when giving the contribution of YHWH to make atonement for your lives. [fn15]

**30:16** And you shall take the silver of the atonements from the Sons of Israel, and you shall give it for the service of the tent of meeting; and it shall be for the Sons of Israel as a memorial before YHWH, to make atonement for your lives." [fn16]

---

## The Bronze Basin for Washing (vv. 17-21)

**30:17** And YHWH spoke to Moses, saying: [fn17]

**30:18** "And you shall make a basin of bronze and its stand of bronze, for washing; and you shall place it between the tent of meeting and the altar, and you shall put water there. [fn18]

**30:19** And Aaron and his sons shall wash from it their hands and their feet. [fn19]

**30:20** When they go into the tent of meeting they shall wash with water and they shall not die, or when they draw near to the altar to minister, to burn an offering by fire to YHWH. [fn20]

**30:21** And they shall wash their hands and their feet and they shall not die; and it shall be for them a statute forever, for him and for his seed throughout their generations." [fn21]

---

## The Holy Anointing Oil (vv. 22-33)

**30:22** And YHWH spoke to Moses, saying: [fn22]

**30:23** "And you, take for yourself the finest spices: flowing myrrh, five hundred shekels; and fragrant cinnamon, half of that -- two hundred and fifty; and fragrant cane, two hundred and fifty; [fn23]

**30:24** and cassia, five hundred, according to the shekel of the sanctuary; and olive oil, a hin. [fn24]

**30:25** And you shall make it an oil of holy anointing, a compound of compounding, the work of a compounder; an oil of holy anointing it shall be. [fn25]

**30:26** And you shall anoint with it the tent of meeting and the ark of the testimony, [fn26]

**30:27** and the table and all its vessels, and the lampstand and its vessels, and the altar of incense, [fn27]

**30:28** and the altar of burnt offering and all its vessels, and the basin and its stand. [fn28]

**30:29** And you shall consecrate them, and they shall be most holy; everything that touches them shall be holy. [fn29]

**30:30** And Aaron and his sons you shall anoint, and you shall consecrate them to serve as priest to me. [fn30]

**30:31** And to the Sons of Israel you shall speak, saying: 'An oil of holy anointing this shall be to me throughout your generations. [fn31]

**30:32** Upon the flesh of a man it shall not be poured, and in its proportion you shall not make any like it; it is holy -- holy it shall be to you. [fn32]

**30:33** A man who compounds like it, or who puts of it upon an outsider, shall be cut off from his people.'" [fn33]

---

## The Holy Incense (vv. 34-38)

**30:34** And YHWH said to Moses: "Take for yourself spices -- stacte and onycha and galbanum -- spices and pure frankincense; part for part it shall be. [fn34]

**30:35** And you shall make it an incense, a compound, the work of a compounder, salted, pure, holy. [fn35]

**30:36** And you shall grind some of it fine, and you shall place some of it before the testimony in the tent of meeting, where I will meet with you there; most holy it shall be to you. [fn36]

**30:37** And the incense which you shall make -- in its proportion you shall not make it for yourselves; holy it shall be to you, for YHWH. [fn37]

**30:38** A man who makes like it to smell it shall be cut off from his people." [fn38]

---

## Translator's Notes

### Structural Note

Exodus 30 gathers five distinct prescriptions that complete the tabernacle system inaugurated in chapters 25-29. The incense altar (vv. 1-10), the census ransom (vv. 11-16), the bronze basin (vv. 17-21), the anointing oil (vv. 22-33), and the sacred incense (vv. 34-38) supply the remaining instruments and substances without which priestly worship cannot proceed. Their placement after the ordination chapter (29) rather than alongside the furnishing instructions (25-27) has generated scholarly discussion; the most persuasive explanation is that these items presuppose the priesthood just consecrated -- the incense altar requires a priest, the basin requires priests who must wash, and the anointing oil requires a priesthood to anoint. The five sections are linked by the theme of atonement and consecration: the incense altar receives annual atonement (v. 10); the census tax is a *kofer* ("ransom") effecting *kippurim* ("atonements," v. 16); the basin provides purification on pain of death (vv. 20-21); the anointing oil consecrates every sacred object and person (vv. 26-30); and the incense fills the holy place with the fragrance of worship. Together they establish the conditions under which the priestly service described in Leviticus can begin.

### Key Decisions

**"A place of burning incense" for *miqtar qetoret* (v. 1):** EMET renders "a place of burning incense." LOGOS renders "for the burning of incense." PNEUMA renders "for burning incense." The consensus adopts EMET's rendering -- "a place of burning incense" preserves the nominal force of *miqtar* as a noun of place from *qatar* ("to turn to smoke"), distinguishing this altar's sole function.

**"Unauthorized" for *zarah* (v. 9):** EMET renders "strange." LOGOS renders "unauthorized." PNEUMA renders "unauthorized." The consensus follows the LOGOS/PNEUMA majority -- "unauthorized" conveys the functional sense of *zarah* (from *zur*, "to be a stranger") in a cultic context more clearly than "strange," which in modern English suggests oddity rather than illegitimacy.

**"Sin offering of atonement" for *chattat ha-kippurim* (v. 10):** EMET renders "sin offering of atonement." LOGOS renders "sin offering of atonement." PNEUMA renders "annual purification offering." The consensus follows EMET/LOGOS -- "sin offering" is consistent with the decision at 29:14, and "atonement" preserves the *kippurim* element. PNEUMA's "purification offering" captures the piel function but obscures the *chet*/*chattat* connection.

**"Take the sum" for *tissa et-rosh* (v. 12):** EMET renders "take up the head." LOGOS renders "take the sum." PNEUMA renders "take a census." The consensus follows LOGOS -- "take the sum" preserves the *nasa et-rosh* idiom ("lift the head") in a way that communicates the census meaning without requiring explanation, while being more faithful than PNEUMA's fully interpretive "take a census."

**"Ransom for his life" for *kofer nafsho* (v. 12):** EMET renders "ransom of his soul." LOGOS renders "ransom for his soul." PNEUMA renders "ransom for his life." The consensus follows PNEUMA -- "ransom for his life" best conveys the meaning of *nefesh* in this context, where the concern is the physical life endangered by the census. "Soul" in modern English suggests the immaterial self, whereas *nefesh* here denotes the whole living person.

**"Contribution" for *terumah* (vv. 13-16):** All three translators agree on "contribution." The consensus follows unanimously. *Terumah* (from *rum*, "to raise") is a "raised offering" or contribution lifted up to YHWH.

**"Basin" for *kiyyor* (v. 18):** EMET renders "basin." LOGOS renders "basin." PNEUMA renders "basin." The consensus follows unanimously.

**"Minister" for *le-sharet* (v. 20):** EMET renders "to minister." LOGOS renders "to minister." PNEUMA renders "to serve." The consensus follows EMET/LOGOS -- "minister" preserves the technical sacral register of *sharet* (piel of *sharat*, "to serve in a sacred capacity"), distinguishing it from *avad* ("to serve, work") in ordinary contexts.

**"The finest spices" / "spices of the head" for *besamim rosh* (v. 23):** EMET renders "spices of the head." LOGOS renders "spices of the finest." PNEUMA renders "the finest spices." The consensus adopts "the finest spices" -- *rosh* ("head, chief") functions here as a superlative adjective. EMET's "spices of the head" is overly literal and unclear in English.

**"Flowing myrrh" for *mor-deror* (v. 23):** EMET renders "myrrh of freedom." LOGOS renders "flowing myrrh." PNEUMA renders "flowing myrrh." The consensus follows LOGOS/PNEUMA -- "flowing myrrh" conveys the practical meaning of *deror* ("freedom, free-flowing") in this context: the finest grade that drips freely from the tree without pressing.

**"Compound of compounding, the work of a compounder" for *roqakh mirqakhat ma'aseh roqeakh* (v. 25):** EMET renders "compound of compounding, the work of a compounder." LOGOS renders "perfume mixture, the work of a perfumer." PNEUMA renders "a perfumer's blend." The consensus follows EMET -- "compound/compounder" preserves the root *r-q-kh* ("to mix, compound") and the trilingual wordplay of *roqakh/mirqakhat/roqeakh* without importing the English word "perfumer," which carries connotations of cosmetic fragrance rather than sacred compounding.

**"Vessels" for *kelim* (vv. 27-28):** EMET renders "vessels." LOGOS renders "utensils." PNEUMA renders "utensils." The consensus follows EMET -- "vessels" is the standard biblical translation of *kelim* and maintains consistency with the tabernacle vocabulary established in earlier chapters.

**"Outsider" for *zar* (v. 33):** EMET renders "stranger." LOGOS renders "stranger." PNEUMA renders "outsider." The consensus follows PNEUMA -- "outsider" conveys the meaning of *zar* (one who is not authorized, not of the priestly line) more precisely than "stranger," which in modern English suggests a foreigner rather than an unauthorized person.

**"To serve as priest to me" for *le-khahen li* (v. 30):** Consistent with the decision at 28:1 and 29:1. EMET renders "to be priests to me." LOGOS renders "to minister as priests to Me." PNEUMA renders "to serve me as priests." The consensus adopts "to serve as priest to me."

**"Statute forever" for *khoq olam* (v. 21):** Consistent with the decision at 27:21 and 29:9. EMET renders "statute of perpetuity." LOGOS renders "perpetual statute." PNEUMA renders "lasting ordinance." The consensus adopts "statute forever."

**"Part for part" for *bad be-vad* (v. 34):** EMET renders "part for part." LOGOS renders "part for part." PNEUMA renders "each in equal amounts." The consensus follows EMET/LOGOS -- "part for part" preserves the Hebrew idiom *bad be-vad* and its distinctive equal-proportions formula.

**"Salted" for *memullakh* (v. 35):** EMET renders "salted." LOGOS renders "salted." PNEUMA renders "seasoned with salt." The consensus follows EMET/LOGOS -- "salted" directly renders the hophal participle *memullakh* (from *malakh*, "to salt") without the explanatory expansion.

**"Grind" for *shakhaq* (v. 36):** EMET renders "beat." LOGOS renders "grind." PNEUMA renders "grind." The consensus follows LOGOS/PNEUMA -- "grind" more accurately conveys the action of reducing the incense to fine powder. *Shakhaq* means "to pulverize, grind fine."

**"Cut off" for *nikhrat* (vv. 33, 38):** All three translators agree on "cut off." The consensus follows unanimously. The niphal of *karat* ("to cut") denotes the penalty of *karet* -- excision from the community.

### Vaticanus (LXX) Divergences

The Vaticanus text of Exodus 30 (pages 73-74) follows the MT closely in structure and content. Notable features: (1) At v. 1, the LXX renders *mizbeakh miqtar qetoret* as *thysiastērion thymiamatos* ("altar of incense"). (2) At v. 10, the annual atonement is rendered *exilasetai Aarōn epi tōn keratōn autou hapax tou eniautou apo tou haimatos tou katharismou* ("Aaron shall make propitiation upon its horns once in the year from the blood of purification"), where *katharismos* ("purification") interprets the MT's *chattat ha-kippurim*. (3) At v. 12, the census formula *ki tissa et-rosh* is rendered *ean labēs ton synlogismon tōn huiōn Israēl* ("when you take the reckoning of the sons of Israel"), replacing the literal "lifting of the head" with the more interpretive "reckoning." (4) At v. 13, the half-shekel is rendered *to hēmisy tou didrachmou* ("half of the didrachma"), converting the Hebrew weight into the Greek monetary equivalent -- a conversion that stands behind the "temple tax" (*didrachma*) of Matthew 17:24-27. (5) At v. 23, the anointing oil ingredients are rendered with Greek spice terminology: *smyrna eklektē* ("choice myrrh") for *mor-deror*, *kinamōmon euōdes* ("fragrant cinnamon"), *kalamos euōdes* ("fragrant cane"). (6) At v. 34, the incense ingredients use *staktēn* ("stacte"), *onycha*, *chalbanēn* ("galbanum"), and *libanon diaphanē* ("transparent frankincense"), with the equal-proportions formula rendered *ison isō estai* ("equal to equal it shall be"). (7) The *karet* formula *ve-nikhrat me-ammav* (vv. 33, 38) is rendered *exolethreuthēsetai ek tou laou autou* ("shall be utterly destroyed from his people"), using a stronger verb than simple "cutting off." (8) The LXX consistently renders YHWH as *kyrios* throughout.

---

> **[fn1] v.1 -- *Ve-asita mizbeakh miqtar qetoret atsei shittim ta'aseh oto*: "And you shall make an altar, a place of burning incense; of acacia wood you shall make it"** -- EMET renders "a place of burning incense." LOGOS renders "for the burning of incense." PNEUMA renders "for burning incense." The consensus follows EMET -- *miqtar* is a noun of place from *qatar* ("to turn to smoke"), designating the altar's sole function. The incense altar is distinct from the bronze altar of burnt offering in material (gold-overlaid wood rather than bronze), size (one cubit square and two cubits high, far smaller), location (inside the tabernacle proper), and function (burning fragrant incense rather than animal sacrifices).

> **[fn2] v.2 -- *Ammah orkho ve-ammah rokhbo ravua' yihyeh ve-ammatayim qomato mimmenu qarnotav*: "A cubit its length and a cubit its width -- square it shall be -- and two cubits its height; from it shall be its horns"** -- All three translators agree in substance. *Mimmenu qarnotav* ("from it, its horns") -- the horns are integral to the altar, carved from the same piece of wood, not attached separately. The dimensions (approximately 1.5 feet square by 3 feet tall) make this the smallest altar in the tabernacle.

> **[fn3] v.3 -- *Ve-tsippita oto zahav tahor et-gago ve-et-qirotav saviv ve-et-qarnotav ve-asita lo zer zahav saviv*: "And you shall overlay it with pure gold -- its top and its walls all around and its horns -- and you shall make for it a molding of gold all around"** -- All three translators agree. *Zer* ("molding, border crown") -- the same decorative rim of gold used for the ark (25:11) and the table (25:24).

> **[fn4] v.4 -- *U-shtei tabba'ot zahav ta'aseh-lo mi-takhat le-zero al shnei tsala'ov ta'aseh al-shnei tsiddav ve-hayah le-vattim le-vaddim laset oto ba-hemah*: "And two rings of gold you shall make for it beneath its molding; on its two sides you shall make them, on its two flanks; and they shall be holders for poles to carry it by them"** -- All three translators agree. *Le-vattim* ("as holders, as housings") -- from *bayit* ("house"); the rings serve as receptacles for the carrying poles.

> **[fn5] v.5 -- *Ve-asita et-ha-baddim atsei shittim ve-tsippita otam zahav*: "And you shall make the poles of acacia wood, and you shall overlay them with gold"** -- All three translators agree. The same carrying apparatus specified for the ark (25:13) and the table (25:28).

> **[fn6] v.6 -- *Ve-natattah oto lifnei ha-parokhet asher al-aron ha-edut lifnei ha-kapporet asher al-ha-edut asher ivvad lekha shammah*: "And you shall place it before the veil which is over the ark of the testimony, before the mercy seat which is over the testimony, where I will meet with you there"** -- All three translators agree. *Ha-kapporet* ("the mercy seat") -- from *kaphar* ("to cover, atone"); the incense altar stands just outside the veil, directly before the place of divine meeting. The threefold *asher* clause chains veil to ark to mercy seat to divine appointment, locating the altar at the threshold of the unapproachable.

> **[fn7] v.7 -- *Ve-hiqtir alav Aharon qetoret sammim ba-boqer ba-boqer be-heitivo et-ha-nerot yaqtirenah*: "And Aaron shall burn upon it fragrant incense; morning by morning, when he tends the lamps, he shall burn it"** -- EMET renders "incense of spices." LOGOS renders "fragrant incense." PNEUMA renders "fragrant incense." The consensus follows LOGOS/PNEUMA -- "fragrant incense" conveys *qetoret sammim* in natural English. *Ba-boqer ba-boqer* ("morning by morning") -- the Hebrew doubles the word for emphasis, meaning "every morning"; the incense is synchronized with the lamp-tending.

> **[fn8] v.8 -- *U-ve-ha'alot Aharon et-ha-nerot bein ha-arbayim yaqtirenah qetoret tamid lifnei YHWH le-doroteikhem*: "And when Aaron lights the lamps between the two evenings, he shall burn it -- a perpetual incense before YHWH throughout your generations"** -- EMET renders "between the evenings." LOGOS renders "between the two evenings." PNEUMA renders "at twilight." The consensus follows LOGOS -- consistent with the decision at 29:39. *Qetoret tamid* ("perpetual incense") -- *tamid* marks this as a continual ordinance, parallel to the *olat tamid* ("continual burnt offering") of 29:42. PNEUMA renders "so that incense rises before YHWH regularly" -- interpretive but not retained.

> **[fn9] v.9 -- *Lo-ta'alu alav qetoret zarah ve-olah u-minkhah ve-nesekh lo tissekhu alav*: "You shall not offer upon it unauthorized incense, nor a burnt offering, nor a grain offering; and a drink offering you shall not pour upon it"** -- EMET renders "strange." LOGOS renders "unauthorized." PNEUMA renders "unauthorized." The consensus follows LOGOS/PNEUMA (see Key Decisions). *Zarah* ("unauthorized, foreign") -- from *zur* ("to be a stranger"); any incense other than the prescribed formula (vv. 34-38) is *zarah*; the prohibition is absolute and will be fatally violated by Nadab and Abihu (Lev 10:1).

> **[fn10] v.10 -- *Ve-khipper Aharon al-qarnotav akhat ba-shanah mi-dam chattat ha-kippurim akhat ba-shanah yekhapper alav le-doroteikhem qodesh-qodashim hu la-YHWH*: "And Aaron shall make atonement upon its horns once in the year; from the blood of the sin offering of atonement, once in the year he shall make atonement upon it throughout your generations; it is most holy to YHWH"** -- All three translators agree in substance. PNEUMA renders "annual purification offering" for *chattat ha-kippurim*; the consensus preserves "sin offering of atonement" consistent with the decision at 29:14. *Qodesh qodashim* ("most holy") -- the superlative construction places this small golden altar in the highest category of holiness. The annual blood-purification refers to Yom Kippur (Lev 16:18-19).

> **[fn11] v.11 -- *Va-yedabber YHWH el-Mosheh le'mor*: "And YHWH spoke to Moses, saying"** -- All three translators agree. A fresh divine speech formula marking a new section.

> **[fn12] v.12 -- *Ki tissa et-rosh benei-Yisrael li-fequdeihem ve-natenu ish kofer nafsho la-YHWH bi-fqod otam ve-lo-yihyeh vahem negef bi-fqod otam*: "When you take the sum of the Sons of Israel according to those who are numbered of them, then each man shall give a ransom for his life to YHWH when you number them, and there shall not be a plague among them when you number them"** -- EMET renders "take up the head." LOGOS renders "take the sum." PNEUMA renders "take a census." The consensus follows LOGOS (see Key Decisions). *Kofer nafsho* ("ransom for his life") -- from *kaphar* ("to cover, ransom"); the *kofer* is a protective payment that covers each life before YHWH. Counting people exposes them to danger; David's census without the *kofer* provision resulted in plague (2 Sam 24). EMET renders "ransom of his soul"; PNEUMA renders "ransom for his life." The consensus follows PNEUMA for *nefesh* (see Key Decisions).

> **[fn13] v.13 -- *Zeh yittenu kol-ha-over al-ha-pequdim makhatsit ha-sheqel be-sheqel ha-qodesh esrim gerah ha-sheqel makhatsit ha-sheqel terumah la-YHWH*: "This they shall give -- everyone who passes over to those who are numbered -- a half-shekel according to the shekel of the sanctuary (twenty gerahs the shekel); a half-shekel as a contribution to YHWH"** -- All three translators agree in substance. *Terumah* ("contribution") -- from *rum* ("to raise"); the half-shekel is literally "lifted up" to YHWH as sacred donation.

> **[fn14] v.14 -- *Kol ha-over al-ha-pequdim mi-ben esrim shanah va-ma'lah yitten terumat YHWH*: "Everyone who passes over to those who are numbered, from a son of twenty years and upward, shall give the contribution of YHWH"** -- All three translators agree. *Mi-ben esrim shanah* ("from a son of twenty years") -- the age of military and civic responsibility; the same threshold for the census in Numbers 1:3.

> **[fn15] v.15 -- *He-ashir lo-yarbeh ve-ha-dal lo yam'it mi-makhatsit ha-sheqel latet et-terumat YHWH le-khapper al-nafshoteikhem*: "The rich shall not give more and the poor shall not give less than the half-shekel, when giving the contribution of YHWH to make atonement for your lives"** -- All three translators agree in substance. *He-ashir... ve-ha-dal* ("the rich... and the poor") -- every soul is of equal value before YHWH; the ransom is identical regardless of wealth.

> **[fn16] v.16 -- *Ve-laqakhta et-kesef ha-kippurim me-et benei Yisrael ve-natattah oto al-avodat ohel mo'ed ve-hayah li-vnei Yisrael le-zikkaron lifnei YHWH le-khapper al-nafshoteikhem*: "And you shall take the silver of the atonements from the Sons of Israel, and you shall give it for the service of the tent of meeting; and it shall be for the Sons of Israel as a memorial before YHWH, to make atonement for your lives"** -- All three translators agree. *Le-zikkaron* ("as a memorial") -- from *zakhar* ("to remember"); the silver funds the tabernacle service while serving as a remembrance before YHWH of each counted life.

> **[fn17] v.17 -- *Va-yedabber YHWH el-Mosheh le'mor*: "And YHWH spoke to Moses, saying"** -- All three translators agree. A third divine speech formula in this chapter.

> **[fn18] v.18 -- *Ve-asita kiyyor nekhoshet ve-khanno nekhoshet le-rakhtsah ve-natattah oto bein-ohel mo'ed u-vein ha-mizbeakh ve-natattah shammah mayim*: "And you shall make a basin of bronze and its stand of bronze, for washing; and you shall place it between the tent of meeting and the altar, and you shall put water there"** -- All three translators agree. *Kiyyor* ("basin, laver") -- positioned between the tent and the altar so priests must wash before entering either the holy place or approaching the altar. Unlike other tabernacle furnishings, the *kiyyor* receives no dimensions -- only its material, its stand, and its function are specified.

> **[fn19] v.19 -- *Ve-rakhatsu Aharon u-vanav mimmenu et-yedeihem ve-et-ragleihem*: "And Aaron and his sons shall wash from it their hands and their feet"** -- All three translators agree. The extremities that handle sacred objects and walk on holy ground -- the same points marked with blood in 29:20.

> **[fn20] v.20 -- *Be-vo'am el-ohel mo'ed yirkhatsu-mayim ve-lo yamutu o be-gishtem el-ha-mizbeakh le-sharet le-haqtir ishsheh la-YHWH*: "When they go into the tent of meeting they shall wash with water and they shall not die, or when they draw near to the altar to minister, to burn an offering by fire to YHWH"** -- All three translators agree in substance. *Ve-lo yamutu* ("and they shall not die") -- the death penalty for unwashed approach underscores that holiness is a matter of life and death. EMET renders *le-haqtir* as "to burn"; the consensus retains "to burn" here since the context is general ministerial action, not the technical *hiqtir*-as-"turn to smoke" distinction reserved for the altar's sacrificial smoke.

> **[fn21] v.21 -- *Ve-rakhatsu yedeihem ve-ragleihem ve-lo yamutu ve-hayetah lahem khoq-olam lo u-le-zar'o le-dorotam*: "And they shall wash their hands and their feet and they shall not die; and it shall be for them a statute forever, for him and for his seed throughout their generations"** -- All three translators agree in substance. *Khoq-olam* ("statute forever") -- consistent with the decision at 27:21 and 29:9. PNEUMA renders "lasting ordinance." The consensus preserves "statute forever."

> **[fn22] v.22 -- *Va-yedabber YHWH el-Mosheh le'mor*: "And YHWH spoke to Moses, saying"** -- All three translators agree. A fourth divine speech formula, introducing the sacred anointing oil recipe.

> **[fn23] v.23 -- *Ve-attah qakh-lekha besamim rosh mor-deror khamesh me'ot ve-qinnemon-besem makhatsito khamishim u-matayim u-qeneh-vosem khamishim u-matayim*: "And you, take for yourself the finest spices: flowing myrrh, five hundred shekels; and fragrant cinnamon, half of that -- two hundred and fifty; and fragrant cane, two hundred and fifty"** -- EMET renders *besamim rosh* as "spices of the head." LOGOS renders "spices of the finest." PNEUMA renders "the finest spices." The consensus follows PNEUMA (see Key Decisions). EMET renders *mor-deror* as "myrrh of freedom." LOGOS/PNEUMA render "flowing myrrh." The consensus follows LOGOS/PNEUMA (see Key Decisions). *Mor-deror* -- the finest myrrh that flows freely from the tree without incision.

> **[fn24] v.24 -- *Ve-qiddah khamesh me'ot be-sheqel ha-qodesh ve-shemen zayit hin*: "And cassia, five hundred, according to the shekel of the sanctuary; and olive oil, a hin"** -- All three translators agree. *Hin* -- a liquid measure of approximately 6 liters (1.5 gallons); the olive oil serves as the base into which the dry spices are compounded.

> **[fn25] v.25 -- *Ve-asita oto shemen mishkhat-qodesh roqakh mirqakhat ma'aseh roqeakh shemen mishkhat-qodesh yihyeh*: "And you shall make it an oil of holy anointing, a compound of compounding, the work of a compounder; an oil of holy anointing it shall be"** -- EMET renders "compound of compounding, the work of a compounder." LOGOS renders "perfume mixture, the work of a perfumer." PNEUMA renders "a perfumer's blend." The consensus follows EMET (see Key Decisions). *Shemen mishkhat-qodesh* ("oil of holy anointing") -- the compound phrase denotes the sacred oil from which *mashiakh* ("messiah, anointed one") derives. The triple repetition of the *r-q-kh* root (*roqakh/mirqakhat/roqeakh*) emphasizes professional preparation.

> **[fn26] v.26 -- *U-mashakhta vo et-ohel mo'ed ve-et aron ha-edut*: "And you shall anoint with it the tent of meeting and the ark of the testimony"** -- All three translators agree. *U-mashakhta* (qal perfect of *mashakh*) -- the verb from which *mashiakh* ("messiah") derives.

> **[fn27] v.27 -- *Ve-et-ha-shulkhan ve-et-kol-kelav ve-et-ha-menorah ve-et-keleiha ve-et mizbeakh ha-qetoret*: "And the table and all its vessels, and the lampstand and its vessels, and the altar of incense"** -- EMET renders "vessels." LOGOS renders "utensils." PNEUMA renders "utensils." The consensus follows EMET (see Key Decisions).

> **[fn28] v.28 -- *Ve-et mizbeakh ha-olah ve-et-kol-kelav ve-et-ha-kiyyor ve-et-kanno*: "And the altar of burnt offering and all its vessels, and the basin and its stand"** -- All three translators agree.

> **[fn29] v.29 -- *Ve-qiddashta otam ve-hayu qodesh qodashim kol-ha-nogea' bahem yiqdash*: "And you shall consecrate them, and they shall be most holy; everything that touches them shall be holy"** -- All three translators agree. *Kol-ha-nogea' bahem yiqdash* ("everything touching them shall be holy") -- holiness is contagious through contact; the anointed objects transmit their consecrated status.

> **[fn30] v.30 -- *Ve-et-Aharon ve-et-banav timshakh ve-qiddashta otam le-khahen li*: "And Aaron and his sons you shall anoint, and you shall consecrate them to serve as priest to me"** -- EMET renders "to be priests to me." LOGOS renders "to minister as priests to Me." PNEUMA renders "to serve me as priests." The consensus adopts "to serve as priest to me" -- consistent with 28:1 and 29:1.

> **[fn31] v.31 -- *Ve-el-benei Yisrael tedabber le'mor shemen mishkhat-qodesh yihyeh zeh li le-doroteikhem*: "And to the Sons of Israel you shall speak, saying: 'An oil of holy anointing this shall be to me throughout your generations'"** -- All three translators agree. *Li* ("to me") -- the oil belongs to YHWH; it is divine possession entrusted for specific use.

> **[fn32] v.32 -- *Al-besar adam lo yissakh u-ve-matkunato lo ta'asu kamohu qodesh hu qodesh yihyeh lakhem*: "Upon the flesh of a man it shall not be poured, and in its proportion you shall not make any like it; it is holy -- holy it shall be to you"** -- All three translators agree in substance. *Matkunato* ("its proportion, its formula") -- from *takhan* ("to measure, regulate"); the exact recipe is restricted; no one may duplicate the proportions for personal use.

> **[fn33] v.33 -- *Ish asher yirqakh kamohu va-asher yitten mimmenu al-zar ve-nikhrat me-ammav*: "A man who compounds like it, or who puts of it upon an outsider, shall be cut off from his people"** -- EMET renders *zar* as "stranger." LOGOS renders "stranger." PNEUMA renders "outsider." The consensus follows PNEUMA (see Key Decisions). *Ve-nikhrat* (niphal of *karat*) -- the penalty of *karet*, excision from the community; the most severe penalty for ritual violation.

> **[fn34] v.34 -- *Va-yomer YHWH el-Mosheh qakh-lekha sammim nataf u-shkhelet ve-khelbenah sammim u-levonah zakh bad be-vad yihyeh*: "And YHWH said to Moses: 'Take for yourself spices -- stacte and onycha and galbanum -- spices and pure frankincense; part for part it shall be'"** -- All three translators agree in substance. PNEUMA renders *bad be-vad* as "each in equal amounts." The consensus follows EMET/LOGOS with "part for part" (see Key Decisions). *Bad be-vad* -- each ingredient in equal measure; the incense formula requires precise balance among its four components.

> **[fn35] v.35 -- *Ve-asita otah qetoret roqakh ma'aseh roqeakh memullakh tahor qodesh*: "And you shall make it an incense, a compound, the work of a compounder, salted, pure, holy"** -- EMET renders "salted." LOGOS renders "salted." PNEUMA renders "seasoned with salt." The consensus follows EMET/LOGOS (see Key Decisions). *Memullakh* (hophal participle of *malakh*) -- salt is a preservative and covenant symbol (Lev 2:13); its inclusion marks the incense as a covenant substance. The three adjectives form an ascending sequence: from physical preparation (salted) to ritual status (pure) to sacred designation (holy).

> **[fn36] v.36 -- *Ve-shakhaqta mimmennah hadeq ve-natattah mimmennah lifnei ha-edut be-ohel mo'ed asher ivvad lekha shammah qodesh qodashim tihyeh lakhem*: "And you shall grind some of it fine, and you shall place some of it before the testimony in the tent of meeting, where I will meet with you there; most holy it shall be to you"** -- EMET renders "beat." LOGOS renders "grind." PNEUMA renders "grind." The consensus follows LOGOS/PNEUMA (see Key Decisions). *Hadeq* ("fine") -- the incense is ground to a fine powder before use.

> **[fn37] v.37 -- *Ve-ha-qetoret asher ta'aseh be-matkunatah lo ta'asu lakhem qodesh tihyeh lekha la-YHWH*: "And the incense which you shall make -- in its proportion you shall not make it for yourselves; holy it shall be to you, for YHWH"** -- All three translators agree in substance. *Be-matkunatah* ("in its proportion") -- the same term as v. 32 for the oil; the formula is exclusively sacred.

> **[fn38] v.38 -- *Ish asher-ya'aseh kamohah le-hariakh bah ve-nikhrat me-ammav*: "A man who makes like it to smell it shall be cut off from his people"** -- All three translators agree. *Le-hariakh* (hiphil infinitive construct of *riakh*, "to smell") -- the offense is making the incense for personal sensory pleasure; what belongs to YHWH is forbidden to human enjoyment.

---

*Translated from the Leningrad Codex (B19A) with reference to Codex Vaticanus (LXX). All transliterations follow standard academic conventions. Hebrew text follows the Masoretic vocalization.*
